title: Campyloneurum nidulare
taxonomy: Campyloneurum nidulare is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales, a large and diverse group of ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the subclass Polypodiidae, which contains various families of ferns. The order Polypodiales is nested within the subclass Polypodiidae, indicating a close evolutionary relationship. At the kingdom level, Campyloneurum nidulare is classified as a plant within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Campyloneurum nidulare, also known as the false parsley fern, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues such as fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. In some cultures, the plant is used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make tea, which is believed to have medicinal properties.
